description: The Single Source Regulations Office (SSRO) is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Ministry of Defence (MOD). SSRO is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Ministry of Defence .

details.body:
The Single Source Regulations Office (SSRO) is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Ministry of Defence (MOD).

SSRO is an executive non-departmental public body, sponsored by the Ministry of Defence.

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Compliance Bulletin examines how well defence contractors followed the reporting regulations for non-competitive (also known as single source) defence contracts.
ordered_featured_documents.title: SSRO highlights ongoing challenges in reporting against non-competitive defence contracts

ordered_featured_documents.summary: We are consulting on updates to the way that profit rates for defence-related single source contracts are calculated.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Proposed updates to how the starting point for profit rates for vital single source defence contracts are calculated

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The SSRO has published new guidance that will help all stakeholders identify and apply the regulatory framework for single source defence contracts to qualifying sub-contracts.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Helping the MOD and the defence industry navigate the single source regulatory framework for key supply-chain contracts

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The annual statistics bulletin is part of a regular published series of statistical reports, containing analysis of key QDC/QSC information.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Annual qualifying defence contract statistics 2024-25

ordered_featured_documents.summary: The Secretary of State for Defence has announced his determination of the baseline profit rate and capital servicing rates for 2025/26.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Starting  point for agreeing profit rates on crucial defence contracts announced and contract profit rate analysis published

ordered_featured_documents.summary: Quarterly publication of queries received by the SSRO and the responses. If you have any queries about the responses which the SSRO has published, please email helpdesk@ssro.gov.uk. Someone from the SSRO will be able to contact you to discuss your query.
ordered_featured_documents.title: Responses to Commonly Asked Questions

ordered_featured_links.title: I would like to find out about the SSRO and the regulatory framework
I want to agree the price of a qualifying contract and understand allowable costs and profit rates
I need to submit a report and want to check if it will be compliant

details.secondary_corporate_information_pages: Our Personal information charter explains how we treat your personal information. Read our policy on Social media use.

details.body:
The Chair is responsible for:

  • formulating the SSRO Board’s strategy
  • ensuring that the board, in reaching decisions, complies with legislation and takes proper account of guidance provide by the responsible minister or the department
  • delivering high standards of regularity and propriety
  • representing the views of the Board to the general public
  • ensuring that the work of the Board and its members are reviewed and are working effectively
  • ensuring that Board members are fully briefed in terms of appointment, duties, rights and responsibilities
  • ensuring that there is a code of practice for board members in place consistent with the Cabinet Office model code
  • representing the SSRO in discussions with ministers and single source defence suppliers
  • representing the views of the Board to the general public as well as Government and industry stakeholders

The SSRO Chief Executive is responsible for the effective executive leadership and management of the SSRO within a strategic framework agreed by the main board. They ensure the SSRO meets its statutory functions and contractual obligations and operates strictly in accordance with its legal responsibilities.

Specific responsibilities include:

  • acting as accounting officer to the UK Parliament, exercising the duties and responsibilities of the accounting officer as set out in ‘ Managing public money ’
  • managing budget spending, maintaining a full financial account and arranging an annual audit
  • providing overall leadership and management of operational activities to ensure the achievement of SSRO ’s statutory functions and objectives
  • developing clear and achievable financial and corporate plans for the SSRO evaluating and reporting to the board on the overall performance of the SSRO
  • maintaining effective systems of governance and control, including the development of appropriate policies
  • preparing an annual report and accounts for consideration by the SSRO Audit Committee and approval by the SSRO Board representing the SSRO externally with stakeholders, the media and the corporate and public sector
  • ensuring that the board is properly advised and consulted, enabling effective and timely decision making
  • motivating and providing leadership for the senior management team, leading on the recruitment, appointment and management of senior staff
  • driving forward the SSRO ’s policies on equality and diversity, and staff development

The SSRO Chief Regulatory Officer is responsible for: Oversight of reporting system, including compliance and guidance Profit rate methodology and calculation Guidance on allowable costs, contract profit rate steps, reporting, penalties and referrals Expert opinions and determinations Analysis of statutory reports and other data Keeping the legislation under review Corporate planning and strategy Public interest disclosures

The SSRO Non-Executive Members are responsible for:

  • providing independent and constructive challenge
  • establishing the strategic direction of the SSRO (within a policy and resources framework agreed with ministers)
  • overseeing the development and implementation of strategies, plans and priorities
  • overseeing the development and review of key performance targets including financial targets
  • ensuring that the public body complies with all statutory and administrative requirements on the use of public funds

The SSRO Non-executive members are responsible for:

  • providing independent and constructive challenge
  • establishing the strategic direction of the SSRO (within a policy and resources framework agreed with ministers)
  • overseeing the development and implementation of strategies, plans and priorities
  • overseeing the development and review of key performance targets including financial targets
  • ensuring that the public body complies with all statutory and administrative requirements on the use of public funds

The SSRO Non-Executive Members are responsible for:

  • providing independent and constructive challenge
  • establishing the strategic direction of the SSRO (within a policy and resources framework agreed with ministers)
  • overseeing the development and implementation of strategies, plans and priorities
  • overseeing the development and review of key performance targets including financial targets
  • ensuring that the public body complies with all statutory and administrative requirements on the use of public funds

details.description: The helpdesk is the point of contact within the SSRO where parties to qualifying defence contracts or qualifying sub-contracts can ask for clarification, advice or seek further information on all issues related to the work of the SSRO and its statutory functions.
details.title: SSRO helpdesk

details.description: Guidance on referrals is available at: https://www.gov.uk/government/collections/referrals
details.title: Referrals

details.description: Information on the SSRO’s approach to dealing with FOI requests is available at: https://www.gov.uk/guidance/requesting-information-from-the-ssro
details.title: Freedom of Information Act requests

details.description:
The SSRO is a ‘Prescribed Person’ under the Public Interest Disclosure Act (PIDA) and is able to receive qualifying disclosures from whistleblowers who work for industry, the MOD and third parties. 

The SSRO's whistleblowing policy is available at: https://www.gov.uk/government/publications/ssro-whistleblowing-policy
details.title: Public interest disclosures

details.description: If you have a complaint about the SSRO's conduct please contact us on the email above.
details.title: Complaints

The SSRO Chief Operating Officer is responsible for:

  • Oversight of reporting system, including compliance and guidance
  • Profit rate methodology and calculation
  • Guidance on allowable costs and profit rate steps
  • Expert opinions and determinations
  • Analysis of statutory reports and other data
  • Keeping the legislation under review and legal services
  • Corporate planning and strategy
  • Public interest disclosures

The SSRO Director of Regulation and Economics is responsible for:

  • Profit rate methodology and calculation
  • Production of the profit rate assessment guidance (including 6 steps)
  • Analysis of evidence and data
  • VFM studies and briefings
  • On request analysis from the Secretary of State
  • Defined Pricing Structure
  • Management of Defence Contract Analysis and Reporting System

The SSRO’s Director of Legal and Policy is responsible for:

  • Review of Part 2 of the Defence Reform Act 2014 and the single source contract regulations
  • Guidance
  • Responses to referrals under the Act, including expert opinions and determinations
  • Public interest disclosures
  • Stakeholder engagement strategy
  • Corporate planning
  • Legal services to the SSRO
